title: Add `calyx.primitive` operator
body:
The Calyx `primitive` operator is used to describe properties of verilog externs that the compiler links the final design to. This would be a useful operator to add into the Calyx dialect so that we can start emitting full programs with externals precisely described in CIRCT.

There are two parts to this: (1) The `extern` region that points to a string path which contains all the Verilog definition and contains the shims for all the `primitive` definition in it, and (2) the `primitive` operator itself which is very similar to a `component` except that it allows for parameters. I think it can be very similar to `hw.module` style parameterization.

For example, see the [core primitives file](https://github.com/cucapra/calyx/blob/master/primitives/core.futil) in Calyx.
